title: [ATLAS] KEI-239 — `bd complete` becomes the canonical completion gesture: auto-writes verification + propagates to Linear mirror
description:
## Policy (Dave directive 2026-05-19)

bd is the ideal agent interface. `bd complete <id>` should be the one-shot agents use to mark work done — no separate calls to write `task_verifications` rows, no manual Postgres mutation.

## Fix

In `scripts/tasks_cli.py` `cmd_complete`:

* Accept (existing) `--evidence` flag from KEI-89 Gate 2
* If evidence is provided: write the `task_verifications` row from evidence (existing path)
* If evidence is NOT provided AND `--auto-verify` flag passed: write a synthetic verification row attributed to the calling callsign with behavioral_test='bd complete auto-verify' + test_output='<verbatim PR merge commit SHA + Linear KEI URL>'
* Then proceed with the existing UPDATE `status='done'` flow
* Trigger passes because verification exists; sync_event fires from Postgres trigger; orchestrator mirrors to Linear

## Acceptance

- [ ] `bd complete <id> --auto-verify` writes synthetic verification + sets done
- [ ] `bd complete <id>` (no flags, no evidence) fails with hint: "pass --evidence or --auto-verify"
- [ ] Tests: auto-verify path, evidence path, no-flag failure
- [ ] Depends on KEI-235 (orchestrator-side verification fix already in place)
- [ ] Verbatim live test: `bd complete <test-KEI> --auto-verify` → Postgres status=done → orchestrator → Linear=Done within 30s
- [ ] Lint clean

## Source

Dave directive 2026-05-19: "bd is the ideal."

title: [ATLAS] KEI-238 — Linear webhook ignores echo from orchestrator's own writes
description:
## Problem

Defense-in-depth against the loop pattern that caused the 59-KEI downgrade incident: even with KEI-236 locking the orchestrator to one-way pushes, the Linear webhook currently accepts ALL state changes — including the ones echoed back from the orchestrator's own `issueUpdate` calls. If we ever re-introduce a non-postgres write path, this would re-create the loop.

## Fix

In `src/api/webhooks/linear.py`:

* Parse `data.actor.id` from the Linear webhook payload
* Compare to `LINEAR_VIEWER_ID` env var (the API key's user id)
* If `actor.id == viewer.id` AND `action='update'` AND we're handling a state-change: SKIP (don't write to Postgres, don't emit sync_event)
* Still log + heartbeat as `ignored_self_echo` so the monitor can see the protection working

## Acceptance

- [ ] `_normalise_event` returns None for `action='update'` with `actor.id == LINEAR_VIEWER_ID`
- [ ] LINEAR_VIEWER_ID env var documented + set via `scripts/sync_linear_viewer_env.py` helper (one-line GraphQL `{ viewer { id } }`)
- [ ] Tests: self-echo skipped; other-actor preserved
- [ ] Heartbeat status='ignored_self_echo' on the skip path
- [ ] Live --apply verify: orchestrator-triggered Linear updates don't loop back to Postgres
- [ ] Lint clean

## Source

Dave directive 2026-05-19 — defense in depth.

title: [ATLAS] KEI-237 — reconciler stops emitting Linear→Postgres field_drift events
description:
## Policy (Dave directive 2026-05-19)

Linear is a read-only mirror. The reconciler's `field_drift` bucket — added in KEI-233 — propagates Linear's canonical state DOWN to Postgres. Under the new policy this direction is wrong: Postgres is canonical, Linear is downstream.

## Fix

In `reconcile_three_stores.detect_drift`:

* Keep the `field_drift` bucket detection (still useful for visibility)
* In `_emit_events`, change the emission policy:
  * field_drift entries now emit `origin=postgres, event_type=update` with the Postgres-side payload
  * The orchestrator then dispatches to bd + linear (Linear gets the mirror update)
  * Effect: Postgres's truth flows OUT to Linear, never the reverse

## Acceptance

- [ ] `_emit_events` field_drift branch reads pg_row (not linear_iss) for canonical payload
- [ ] Origin tag flipped to `postgres`
- [ ] Tests: assert emitted origin = postgres
- [ ] Live --apply verify: any remaining field_drift cases push Postgres-state to Linear (not vice versa)
- [ ] Depends on KEI-236 (Linear-as-mirror policy must be locked first)
- [ ] Lint clean

## Source

Dave directive 2026-05-19 + KEI-236 policy lock.

title: [ATLAS] K4-followup — reconciler detects field-level drift, not just missing-store drift
description:
## Problem

Cross-store audit 2026-05-19 revealed **45 stale state mismatches + 4 NULL-status rows** between Linear and Postgres. Linear says `completed`, Postgres still says `available` (etc.). The K4 reconciler (`scripts/reconcile_three_stores.py`) does NOT catch this drift — it only classifies issues into 4 buckets (`in_all_three` / `missing_postgres` / `missing_bd` / `missing_linear`) and only emits sync_events for the three "missing" buckets. KEIs present in all three stores but with diverging field values get no event raised.

## Sample of the drift

```
KEI-1    Linear=canceled   pg=available     "Get familiar with Linear"
KEI-10   Linear=completed  pg=available     "System Health Monitoring Layer"
KEI-209  Linear=completed  pg=available     "[WORKER] Dispatcher — auth_minter"
KEI-218  Linear=started    pg=available     "fleet-supervisor crash-loop fix"
... (49 total)
```

## Fix

Extend `detect_drift` to add a 5th bucket — `field_drift` — for KEIs present in all 3 stores but with diverging `status` (and optionally `title` / `priority`). Treat Linear as canonical (it's the human board). For each row in the field_drift bucket, `_emit_events` raises `sync_events(origin='linear', event_type='update')` with the canonical payload. K3 sync_orchestrator drains and dispatches the corrections to Postgres + bd.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `detect_drift` adds `field_drift` bucket; existing 4 buckets unchanged
- [ ] `_emit_events` emits `update` events from `origin=linear` for each field-drift row
- [ ] Unit tests for `field_drift` classification (status differs, title differs, all-match, ignored statuses)
- [ ] Live `--apply` run on current production state → ≥49 update events emitted → orchestrator drains them → audit re-runs clean
- [ ] `ruff check && ruff format --check` clean
- [ ] KEI-108 gate (no new service files)
- [ ] Dual-approve / Dave-authorised single-merge

## Source

Atlas cross-store audit 2026-05-19. Dave directive: "ship pr".

title: [ORION] NATS — create 4 new streams (elliot.inbox + dispatch.* + review.* + audit) + local-outbox drain daemon
description: Per NATS architecture ratified 2026-05-19 (aiden session 1a5502e8 turn 25, Dave directive precedence). Scope: (1) Create JetStream streams: keiracom.elliot.inbox (all agents → elliot Slack funnel), keiracom.dispatch.<callsign> (elliot → named worker), keiracom.review.<pr_number> (deliberator review threads, open on PR webhook), keiracom.audit (append-only governance trace). All file storage, retention per type (24h ephemeral for status/dispatch, 30d for review/audit). (2) Local-outbox drain daemon: each agent has local outbox dir, daemon drains to NATS on reconnect when NATS unavailable (fail-open pattern). Acceptance: nats stream ls shows all 4 new streams; publish to keiracom.dispatch.atlas; subscribe + receive verified; outbox drains after restart. Linked to existing KEI Agency_OS-zgxtgc (parent supervisor v2).

title: [ATLAS] NATS account ACLs + tg shim for non-elliot callsigns → keiracom.elliot.inbox
description: Per NATS architecture ratified 2026-05-19. Scope: (1) Create NATS user accounts per callsign with subject-pattern ACLs — workers can publish to keiracom.dispatch.<self> + keiracom.agent.status.<self> + keiracom.pair.<own_pair>, can subscribe to keiracom.dispatch.<self>; cannot publish to keiracom.dispatch.* or keiracom.audit. Elliot account has broader write (dispatch.*, audit). (2) Modify tg shim and slack_relay.py callsign guard: non-elliot calls redirect message to NATS publish on keiracom.elliot.inbox (with from-callsign envelope) instead of denying. Result: Aiden/Max/etc say tg msg → arrives in elliot inbox via NATS, never Slack. Acceptance: tg from atlas produces NATS message on elliot.inbox; nats sub keiracom.elliot.inbox receives it; Slack post count from non-elliot callsigns drops to zero.

title: [ATLAS] External Knowledge Ingester — Weaviate ExternalKnowledge + 14-source weekly
description: Generic external-knowledge ingester. Crawl 14 docs sources + GitHub repos, rule-chunk, upsert to new Weaviate ExternalKnowledge collection via text2vec-openai. Weekly systemd timer. Single PR, dual approve. Full spec at Linear KEI-232 (note: collision with my earlier bd-only K2.5-followup external_ref — clean up later).

title: [ATLAS] External Knowledge Ingester — Weaviate ExternalKnowledge collection + 14-source weekly refresh
description:
## Why

Agents reimplement third-party patterns from scratch every session by reading external docs inline. Pre-loading external documentation into Weaviate gives "one brain, all arms" — any agent queries `ExternalKnowledge` and gets answers instantly.

## Build

`scripts/orchestrator/external_knowledge_ingester.py` — generic ingester accepting URLs or GitHub repos. Rule-based chunker (no LLM calls). Weaviate POST via the existing `indexer_base.py` helpers (`ensure_class`, `post_object`, `aggregate_count`, `deterministic_uuid`).

## Weaviate schema — `ExternalKnowledge`

* `url` TEXT
* `source_name` TEXT
* `section_title` TEXT
* `content` TEXT (vectorized via text2vec-openai)
* `chunk_index` INT
* `ingested_at` DATE

Class-level vectorizer: `text2vec-openai` (matches existing Codebase/Decisions/Keis pattern). Deterministic UUID via `deterministic_uuid("external_knowledge", f"{url}#{chunk_index}")`.

## First-batch sources (14)

| Domain | Type | Target |
| -- | -- | -- |
| NATS | docs | [https://docs.nats.io](<https://docs.nats.io>) |
| NATS | repo | [https://github.com/nats-io/nats.py](<https://github.com/nats-io/nats.py>) |
| NATS | repo | [https://github.com/nats-io/nats-architecture-and-design](<https://github.com/nats-io/nats-architecture-and-design>) |
| Valkey | docs | [https://valkey.io/docs](<https://valkey.io/docs>) |
| Valkey | repo | [https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey-py](<https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey-py>) |
| Weaviate | docs | [https://weaviate.io/developers/weaviate](<https://weaviate.io/developers/weaviate>) |
| Weaviate | repo | [https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate-python-client](<https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate-python-client>) |
| Cognee | docs | [https://docs.cognee.ai](<https://docs.cognee.ai>) |
| Cognee | repo | [https://github.com/topoteretes/cognee](<https://github.com/topoteretes/cognee>) |
| LiteLLM | docs | [https://docs.litellm.ai](<https://docs.litellm.ai>) |
| LiteLLM | repo | [https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm](<https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm>) |
| Supabase | docs | [https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth](<https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth>) |
| Supabase | docs | [https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security](<https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security>) |
| Supabase | docs | [https://supabase.com/docs/reference/python/introduction](<https://supabase.com/docs/reference/python/introduction>) |
| FastAPI | docs | [https://fastapi.tiangolo.com](<https://fastapi.tiangolo.com>) |
| asyncpg | docs | [https://magicstack.github.io/asyncpg/current](<https://magicstack.github.io/asyncpg/current>) |
| psycopg3 | docs | [https://www.psycopg.org/psycopg3/docs](<https://www.psycopg.org/psycopg3/docs>) |
| Docker | docs | [https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/ubuntu](<https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/ubuntu>) |
| Docker | docs | [https://docs.docker.com/config/containers/systemd](<https://docs.docker.com/config/containers/systemd>) |
| systemd | docs | [https://systemd.io](<https://systemd.io>) |
| systemd | docs | [https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/systemd.service.html](<https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/systemd.service.html>) |
| Linear API | docs | [https://developers.linear.app/docs](<https://developers.linear.app/docs>) |
| Better Stack | docs | [https://betterstack.com/docs/logs/getting-started](<https://betterstack.com/docs/logs/getting-started>) |
| Better Stack | docs | [https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/webhooks](<https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/webhooks>) |
| Claude Code | docs | [https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/overview](<https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/overview>) |

## Constraints

* No Claude/LLM calls during ingestion — rule-based chunker only (H2/H3 boundary + 2000-char size cap)
* Failed URLs **logged, not silently skipped** — verbatim per-source failure report
* Idempotent — same URL + chunk_index → same Weaviate UUID
* Same chunking + embedding + upsert pattern as existing indexers

## Deliverables

* `scripts/orchestrator/external_knowledge_ingester.py`
* `infra/systemd/external-knowledge-ingester.service` + `.timer` (weekly)
* `scripts/install_external_knowledge_ingester.sh` (KEI-108 compliance)
* `tests/scripts/test_external_knowledge_ingester.py`
* First-run backfill — verbatim chunk-count per source, all > 0

## Acceptance

- [ ] Collection `ExternalKnowledge` exists in Weaviate (verbatim create output)
- [ ] Backfill chunk count > 0 for each of the 14 sources (verbatim)
- [ ] Failed-URL log present + non-fatal
- [ ] Unit tests for chunker + URL classifier pass (verbatim pytest)
- [ ] `ruff check && ruff format --check` clean (verbatim)
- [ ] KEI-108 install-script gate passes
- [ ] Single PR open with dual-approval gate

## Governance

* LAW XIV: verbatim output throughout
* Do not mark complete until backfill chunk_count > 0 for all sources
* Dual approve before merge (Elliot + Aiden)

## Source

Dave directive 2026-05-19 — "External Knowledge Ingester. One brain, all arms."

title: [ATLAS] K3 — sync_orchestrator drain worker (origin-tagged loop-safe fan-out)
description:
## Problem

`completion-sync-worker.py` only handles the completion sink (`tasks.status='done'|'cancelled'`) and only fans out Postgres → Linear/ceo_memory/drive_manual. It cannot consume `sync_events` from `origin='bd'` or `origin='linear'` and cannot prevent loops (no origin tag honoured).

## Spec — Step 3 of 4

Replace `scripts/orchestrator/completion_sync_worker.py` with `scripts/orchestrator/sync_orchestrator.py`:

```python
def process_event(event):
    origin = event["origin"]
    targets = {"bd", "postgres", "linear"} - {origin}  # never echo to origin (loop prevention)
    for target in targets:
        _dispatch[target](event)  # _dispatch_bd / _dispatch_postgres / _dispatch_linear
```

Behaviour:

1. `SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED FROM public.sync_events WHERE processed=false AND attempts < 3` — batch 20
2. For each event, dispatch to the **OTHER two** stores (origin-tag determines skip)
3. Loop prevention is structural: no echo back to origin (the `sync_events` row created by the destination write must NOT trigger a second fan-out — destinations include an `origin_event_id` reference in their write to break the cycle)
4. Per-target writer:
   * `_dispatch_bd` — invoke `bd update/create/close` via subprocess
   * `_dispatch_postgres` — direct `INSERT … ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` to `public.tasks`
   * `_dispatch_linear` — Linear GraphQL `issueUpdate` mutation (reuse existing `_sink_linear` from completion_sync_worker)
5. Idempotency: dedupe on `(task_id, payload_hash)` — duplicate events skipped
6. Backoff: existing ladder `(1s, 5s, 25s)` retained; abandons after 3 attempts with `error_message`
7. Heartbeat tick on each batch (KEI-91 Gate 4 compliance)

## Acceptance

- [ ] `sync_orchestrator.py` lands; `completion_sync_worker.py` deleted or aliased to backwards-compat shim
- [ ] systemd unit `infra/systemd/sync-orchestrator.service` (replaces completion-sync-worker.service)
- [ ] Origin-tag loop prevention test: insert event with origin=`bd`, observe writes to postgres + linear ONLY, no echo back to bd
- [ ] Idempotency test: re-emit same event, observe single dispatch (no double-write)
- [ ] All existing tests `tests/scripts/test_completion_sync_worker.py` ported and pass
- [ ] `ruff check && ruff format --check` clean
- [ ] PR open, dual-approval gate

## Depends on

* K2 (sync_events table) — must be merged first

## Blocks

None — K4 is independent.

title: [ATLAS] K2 — sync_events table + emit hooks (bd-Dolt + Postgres + Linear webhook)
description:
## Problem

No store currently publishes structured change events that the OTHER stores can consume. Existing sync paths are point-to-point and partial:

* `completion-sync-worker` (KEI-74) only fans out on `tasks.status='done'|'cancelled'` (no in_progress / open / label / priority / title)
* `linear_to_bd.py` webhook only handles `create` + `status` (no field updates)
* `bd linear sync --pull` is one-way (Linear → bd-Dolt)
* Nothing emits when bd-Dolt rows change

## Spec — Step 2 of 4

Create `public.sync_events` audit table — single source of truth for all cross-store changes:

```sql
CREATE TABLE public.sync_events (
  id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  origin        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (origin IN ('bd', 'postgres', 'linear')),
  event_type    TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (event_type IN ('create', 'update', 'close', 'reopen', 'priority_change', 'title_change')),
  task_id       TEXT NOT NULL,       -- canonical Linear KEI-N (post-K1)
  bd_id         TEXT,                -- Agency_OS-xxx if known
  payload       JSONB NOT NULL,      -- full field snapshot
  payload_hash  TEXT GENERATED ALWAYS AS (encode(digest(payload::text, 'sha256'), 'hex')) STORED,
  processed     BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
  attempts      INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_sync_events_unprocessed ON public.sync_events(created_at) WHERE processed = FALSE;
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_sync_events_dedupe ON public.sync_events(task_id, payload_hash) WHERE processed = FALSE;
```

Three emit paths:

1. **bd-Dolt** → `.beads/hooks/post-write.sh` (if Beads supports git-style hook) OR a `bd remember`-driven shim that runs after `bd create/update/close` and inserts via psycopg
2. **Postgres** → generalise `trg_tasks_status_to_sync_queue` to `trg_tasks_to_sync_events` covering INSERT + UPDATE on any column (status / priority / title / description / labels)
3. **Linear webhook** → modify `src/api/webhooks/linear.py` to insert into `public.sync_events` (origin=`linear`) instead of calling `linear_to_bd.py` directly

## Acceptance

- [ ] Migration applied (verbatim)
- [ ] Three emit paths instrumented; each emits a synthetic row when its origin store is touched
- [ ] Unit tests for each emit path (mock the other side)
- [ ] Integration smoke: edit a row in each origin store, observe `sync_events` row landing with correct `origin` tag (verbatim DB query)
- [ ] `ruff check && ruff format --check` clean
- [ ] PR open, dual-approval gate

## Depends on

* K1 (bd_id column) — must be merged first; `sync_events.task_id` joins on the canonical KEI-N established by K1

## Blocks

* K3 (sync_orchestrator drain worker reads `sync_events`)

title: [ATLAS] K1 — ID canonicalisation: public.tasks.bd_id column + backfill (sync foundation)
description:
## Problem

Today's three-store drift between **bd-Dolt**, **Postgres** `public.tasks`, and **Linear** is rooted in an ID-key mismatch:

* bd-Dolt keys on `Agency_OS-xxx` short codes (236 issues)
* Linear keys on `KEI-N` per-team sequence
* Postgres `tasks.id` mixes both: **171** `KEI-*` **rows** (from `reconcile_linear_supabase.py`) + **1** `Agency_OS-*` **row** (from `tasks_cli.py` heartbeat/claim) — no join key between them

Direct evidence (atlas session 2026-05-19): `bd close Agency_OS-50hd4r --evidence -` failed with `psycopg.errors.ForeignKeyViolation: Key (task_id)=(Agency_OS-50hd4r) is not present in table "tasks"`. Falls back to `bd update --status=closed` which closes bd-side only; Postgres trigger never fires; completion never reaches Linear.

## Spec — Step 1 of 4 of bi-directional sync architecture

Add `public.tasks.bd_id` column to carry the bd-Dolt short-code paired with each Linear `KEI-N`:

```sql
ALTER TABLE public.tasks ADD COLUMN bd_id TEXT;
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_tasks_bd_id ON public.tasks(bd_id) WHERE bd_id IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_bd_id ON public.tasks(bd_id);
```

Backfill script `scripts/backfill_tasks_bd_id.py`:

1. Iterate every `public.tasks` row WHERE `bd_id IS NULL AND linear_url IS NOT NULL`
2. Match against `bd list --json` rows by `external_ref` URL — bd's existing pairing key
3. UPDATE Postgres row SET `bd_id = '<Agency_OS-xxx>'`
4. Emit verbatim diff summary: matched / unmatched / dual-claims (one Linear URL → multiple bd IDs)

Update `scripts/tasks_cli.py` writers (lines 153, 525, 549, 698, 741):

* When writing heartbeat/claim/verify rows, write **Linear** `KEI-N` **as primary** `id` if a bd↔Linear pair exists; preserve `bd_id` column with the `Agency_OS-xxx`
* If no Linear pair yet, fall through to current behaviour (write `Agency_OS-xxx`) — `bd_id` field unused on that row
* All future readers join on `bd_id` OR `id` depending on which store they entered from

## Acceptance

- [ ] Migration applied to Supabase project `jatzvazlbusedwsnqxzr` (verbatim `apply_migration` output)
- [ ] Backfill script runs dry-run cleanly + paste verbatim summary (matched/unmatched counts)
- [ ] Backfill `--apply` run paste verbatim diff
- [ ] `tasks_cli.py` writers updated; existing tests `tests/scripts/test_tasks_cli.py` still pass (verbatim pytest output)
- [ ] New test `tests/scripts/test_backfill_tasks_bd_id.py` covers the matching + ambiguous-match cases
- [ ] `ruff check && ruff format --check` clean (verbatim)
- [ ] PR open with `[ATLAS]` tag, dual-approval gate

## Source

Atlas diagnosis 2026-05-19 — drift count: bd 147 active vs Postgres 3 active = 144-issue gap. ID-mismatch is root cause #1 of 5.

## Blocks

K2 (sync_events table), K3 (sync_orchestrator), K4 (reconcile_three_stores) — all downstream depend on stable join key.

title: [BUG] KEI-228 — Nova missing relay-watcher + inbox-watcher systemd services + scripts
description:
## Problem

Nova has nova-agent.service enabled+active but lacks the structured inbox/outbox dispatch channel that every other clone-tier callsign has. Verbatim systemctl inventory:

  atlas-inbox-watcher.service    enabled
  atlas-relay-watcher.service    enabled
  orion-inbox-watcher.service    enabled
  orion-relay-watcher.service    enabled
  scout-inbox-watcher.service    enabled
  scout-relay-watcher.service    disabled
  aiden-relay-watcher.service    disabled
  max-relay-watcher.service      enabled
  (NO nova-inbox-watcher.service)
  (NO nova-relay-watcher.service)

Effect: Nova receives no structured dispatch — only tmux send-keys, which is fragile (relies on cursor position, no acknowledgment, no replay-on-restart). Nova explicitly asked for this.

## Cause

The canonical per-callsign watcher scripts live at /home/elliotbot/clawd/scripts/ (host shared, outside all Agency_OS worktrees). Existing scripts:
  atlas_inbox_watcher.sh    (1954 bytes, 2026-04-26)
  atlas_relay_watcher.sh    (1176 bytes, 2026-04-26)
  orion_inbox_watcher.sh    (1895 bytes, 2026-04-26)
  orion_relay_watcher.sh    (1165 bytes, 2026-04-26)
  scout_inbox_watcher.sh    (2005 bytes, 2026-05-12)

Missing: nova_inbox_watcher.sh, nova_relay_watcher.sh, scout_relay_watcher.sh.

Systemd unit files at ~/.config/systemd/user/ are also host-local (not in repo).

## Fix

1. Copy atlas_inbox_watcher.sh → /home/elliotbot/clawd/scripts/nova_inbox_watcher.sh, sed atlas→nova.
2. Copy atlas_relay_watcher.sh → /home/elliotbot/clawd/scripts/nova_relay_watcher.sh, sed atlas→nova.
3. Copy ~/.config/systemd/user/atlas-inbox-watcher.service → nova-inbox-watcher.service, sed atlas→nova (ExecStart path + Description).
4. Same for relay-watcher.service.
5. systemctl --user daemon-reload && systemctl --user enable --now nova-inbox-watcher.service nova-relay-watcher.service.

## Verification

- systemctl --user status nova-inbox-watcher.service nova-relay-watcher.service: both active (running).
- Write a test dispatch JSON to /tmp/telegram-relay-nova/inbox/test.json; watcher injects into nova tmux pane within ~5s.
- journalctl --user -u nova-inbox-watcher.service -n 20: no errors.

## Out of scope

- Bringing the host-private scripts/units into the Agency_OS repo as canonical templates. Worth doing but separate KEI (cross-callsign structural refactor; would touch all 4 existing watcher pairs).
- scout-relay-watcher.service is currently disabled — separate question about whether it should be enabled or if scout uses a different channel.

## Source

Dispatch from Elliot via outbox 2026-05-19. Nova explicitly requested 'start the relay-watcher service for nova'.
close_reason: Duplicate of Agency_OS-pz58 (filed by Elliot first; same scope: Nova missing inbox+relay watcher scripts + systemd units). Working pz58 instead.

title: [BUG] Nova missing relay-watcher + inbox-watcher systemd services + scripts
description: Discovered 2026-05-19 via Nova self-note 'start the relay-watcher service for nova'. systemctl --user list-unit-files shows nova-inbox-watcher.service + nova-relay-watcher.service DO NOT EXIST; corresponding scripts /home/elliotbot/clawd/scripts/nova_inbox_watcher.sh + nova_relay_watcher.sh DO NOT EXIST. Same fleet-infra gap as the self-claim-loop unit (Agency_OS-wj94 / Linear KEI-226). Impact: Nova cannot receive structured inbox dispatch files from elliot/parent (must rely on tmux send-keys which is brittle); Nova cannot send outbound status posts via outbox→Slack relay (must use other paths). Fix scope: (1) Copy /home/elliotbot/clawd/scripts/atlas_inbox_watcher.sh → nova_inbox_watcher.sh, sed atlas→nova on paths + TMUX_TARGET=nova:0.0; (2) Same for atlas_relay_watcher.sh; (3) Copy /home/elliotbot/.config/systemd/user/atlas-inbox-watcher.service → nova-inbox-watcher.service, sed ATLAS→NOVA + atlas→nova; (4) Same for atlas-relay-watcher.service; (5) systemctl --user daemon-reload && enable+start both nova-*-watcher services; (6) Verify via 'ls /tmp/telegram-relay-nova/inbox' inbox dir exists + relay watcher consuming outbox files. Linear mirror tag: KEI-228.
close_reason: Nova watchers deployed to host. (1) /home/elliotbot/clawd/scripts/nova_inbox_watcher.sh created from atlas template (sed atlas→nova, TMUX_TARGET=nova:0.0). (2) /home/elliotbot/clawd/scripts/nova_relay_watcher.sh created (sed atlas→nova, TMUX_TARGET=elliottbot:0.0 preserved — Elliot is Nova's parent). (3) ~/.config/systemd/user/nova-inbox-watcher.service + nova-relay-watcher.service created. (4) systemctl --user daemon-reload + enable executed. (5) Both services systemctl --user start: Active(running) since 2026-05-19T00:27:49Z, inotifywait subprocesses spawned on /tmp/telegram-relay-nova/{inbox,outbox}. Directories auto-created on script start. bash -n syntax check passed on both scripts before enable. Cross-repo follow-up Agency_OS-b7jv filed for bd↔Linear staleness probe.

title: [BUG] Nova missing from agent-self-claim-loop@<callsign>.service fleet — 6/7 coverage
description: Discovered 2026-05-19 via NATS readiness audit triggered by Dave 'is NATS working' check. systemctl --user list-units shows agent-self-claim-loop@<callsign>.service active for aiden/atlas/elliot/max/orion/scout but NOT nova. Nova was added to the fleet as the 7th callsign in earlier dispatcher work but the corresponding self-claim-loop unit was never instantiated/started/enabled. Impact: nova never publishes keiracom.agent.status.nova to NATS, so the supervisor v2 (KEI-221) routing cannot observe nova state transitions; nova falls back to legacy Slack [READY:nova] pings which are subject to Slack rate-limits. Fix scope: (1) systemctl --user enable agent-self-claim-loop@nova.service, (2) systemctl --user start agent-self-claim-loop@nova.service, (3) verify .env has AGENT_ROUTING_NOVA=v2 (confirmed present already), (4) confirm via pgrep -af 'agent_self_claim_loop.*nova', (5) verify NATS sub keiracom.agent.status.nova receives events on next nova busy→ready transition. (1)(2) executed inline at file time. Linear mirror tag: KEI-226.
close_reason: Nova self-claim-loop service confirmed active. Verbatim: systemctl --user status agent-self-claim-loop@nova.service → 'Active: active (running) since Tue 2026-05-19 00:14:22 UTC; 28min ago. Main PID: 1348329 (bash) /home/elliotbot/clawd/Agency_OS/scripts/orchestrator/agent_self_claim_loop.sh --callsign nova'. 7/7 fleet coverage restored. Closed per Elliot 2026-05-19 correction.

title: [WORKER] Composio OAuth handler + routes — post Dave-action vendor onboarding (KEI-124 follow-up)
description:
## What Implement the handler + routes for KEI-124 Composio OAuth. The data-layer migration `oauth_provider_tokens` + skill scaffold lands in PR (this session). Implementation requires Dave-action prerequisites to complete first. ## Dave-action prerequisites (BLOCKS this bd) Per skills/composio-oauth/SKILL.md §Dave-action prerequisites: 1. Composio.dev account creation (organisation: Agency_OS-scout owner). 2. OAuth app registration on Slack / Linear / GitHub via Composio dashboard with documented scopes. 3. Issue Composio master API key → seed env vars `COMPOSIO_API_KEY` (prod) + `COMPOSIO_API_KEY_DEV` (staging) → record in `elliot_internal.api_keys_ledger`. 4. Configure Composio callback URL: `https://api.keiracom.com/api/v1/composio/callback` (+ staging equivalent). 5. Confirm `CUSTOMER_KEY_ENCRYPTION_KEY` provisioned in both environments. ## Spec (post-unblock) - `src/auth/composio_oauth.py` — handler with `start_oauth_flow(tenant_id, provider) -> {oauth_url, state}` + `finalize_connection(state, composio_connection_id, db) -> UUID` + `store_tokens(...)`. Provider allowlist hard-coded: {'slack','linear','github'}. - `src/api/routes/composio.py` — `POST /api/v1/composio/connect?provider=

` + `GET /api/v1/composio/callback?state=...&composio_connection_id=...` + (optional) `POST /api/v1/composio/webhook` for vendor-side revocation events. - Settings: `composio_api_key`, `composio_base_url`, `composio_callback_url` (read from env, fail-fast if unset in prod). - Router registered in `src/api/main.py`. - Tests: `tests/auth/test_composio_oauth.py` + `tests/api/routes/test_composio.py`. Composio HTTP calls mocked via httpx mock. Cover: happy-path, state-replay, expired-state, vendor-503, provider-not-in-allowlist, revoke-cascade-on-customer-soft-delete. ## Acceptance criteria - [ ] All five Dave-action prerequisites confirmed live in `elliot_internal.api_keys_ledger` — paste verbatim query result before claiming. - [ ] `POST /api/v1/composio/connect?provider=slack` returns `{oauth_url, state}` with valid CSRF state stored in Redis (10-min TTL, mirrors crm.py KEI-309 pattern). - [ ] `GET /api/v1/composio/callback` with valid state + connection_id writes encrypted token row to `oauth_provider_tokens` then 302s to frontend. - [ ] Provider allowlist enforced — 400 on unknown provider. - [ ] Vendor 5xx → handler returns 503 (not 500), logs to `interceptor_events`, surfaces to #ceo via existing alert path. - [ ] Soft-revoke cascade trigger when matching `dispatcher_customers` row is soft-deleted. - [ ] Unit + integration tests pass (paste verbatim pytest output). - [ ] Sonar QG=OK, issues=0 on the PR per dual-Sonar AND-rule (KEI-189).

title: [DRIFT AUDIT] bd in_progress vs merged PR cross-reference — systemic close-on-merge gap
description:
## Problem

bd in_progress state systematically drifts from reality: KEIs remain in_progress for hours/days after the PR delivering them is merged. Observed 4 times in 2026-05-18→19 dispatch cycles alone:

- 2026-05-18 dispatch #1: 70st (PR #1046) + lsyd (PR #1060) — both already merged when proposed as in_progress P1 work.
- 2026-05-18 dispatch #2: hzk5 (PR #1053) + 6suajk (PR #983) — both already merged when proposed as P1 candidates.
- 2026-05-19 drift-audit (Elliot, manual pass): 8 more stale entries — tjgihu/PR#1033, v1e279/PR#1038, 50hd4r/PR#1030, p2b6/PR#1030, qiqvu1/PR#954, 5b0t/PR#1021, 524m/PR#1021, gpis/PR#1025+#994.

## Cost

Every stale entry wastes ≥1 redispatch cycle:
- Dispatch agent (Elliot) writes brief assuming the work is open.
- Worker agent (Atlas/Orion/Scout/Nova) does scrutiny pass + duplicate-work alert.
- Outbox traffic + context burn + no shipped work.

Two of yesterday's four dispatches collapsed to "scrutiny only" because every candidate was already shipped.

## Proposed fix

Cron-able audit script that flags in_progress bd KEIs whose KEI-ID appears in a merged-PR title in the same repo. Cross-tracker check, advisory output, recommends closure.

### Algorithm

```
for kei in bd list --status=in_progress --json:
    kei_ids = extract_kei_ids(kei.title + kei.description + kei.notes)
    for kei_id in kei_ids:
        merged_prs = gh api repos/Keiracom/Agency_OS/pulls?state=closed
                     | filter merged==true AND title contains kei_id
        if merged_prs:
            print(kei.id, kei_id, merged_prs[0].number, merged_prs[0].mergedAt)
            print("  → bd-original close", kei.id, "--reason=PR #N merged DATE")
```

### Scope

- One Python script: `scripts/drift_audit_bd_state.py` — runs locally, takes ~30s for ~50 in_progress KEIs.
- One systemd timer: `systemd/drift_audit_bd_state.timer` — daily 06:00.
- Output goes to `/home/elliotbot/clawd/logs/drift_audit.log` + posts a summary to #execution if any drift found.
- Does NOT auto-close. Advisory only; human ratifies via bd-original close.

### Out of scope

- Reverse direction (merged PR with no matching in_progress KEI — that's coverage gap, separate audit).
- Linear sync (bd↔Linear is a separate worker — Agency_OS-9kfy + Agency_OS-iosu).

## Acceptance

- [ ] `scripts/drift_audit_bd_state.py` exists + has unit tests.
- [ ] Dry-run on 2026-05-19 surfaces all 8 entries listed above.
- [ ] systemd timer installed; first run completes successfully on host.
- [ ] If drift > 0, posts a one-line summary to #execution outbox.

## Source

Pattern observed by Atlas across 4 redispatch cycles 2026-05-18→19. Proposed by Atlas to Elliot via outbox 2026-05-19; Elliot ratified filing this KEI as P1.

title: ceo_memory_indexer.py — same prepare_threshold=None bug as PR #1046
description: Decisions count stuck at 300 across multiple restarts even after PR #1046 (prepare_threshold=None for elliot_memories_indexer via indexer_base.run_db_indexer). Root cause: ceo_memory_indexer.py line 160 has its OWN psycopg.connect(_dsn(), autocommit=True) call, NOT routed through indexer_base.run_db_indexer. PR #1046's fix doesn't reach this file. Apply the same one-line fix at line 160: psycopg.connect(_dsn(), autocommit=True, prepare_threshold=None). Same pattern as PR #1046 + same reference_psycopg_supabase_pgbouncer memory. Add to existing PR #1046's regression test family OR write parallel test. Expected effect post-fix: Decisions count climbs 300 → real ceo_memory key count within ~3 min after restart.
notes: PR #1053 open — one-line fix at ceo_memory_indexer.py:160 (psycopg.connect prepare_threshold=None). Same pattern as PR #1046 at second site (ceo_memory has own main(), bypassed indexer_base.run_db_indexer). Empirically confirmed pre-fix: ceo-memory-indexer.log bleeding identical InvalidSqlStatementName loop as elliot-memories-indexer was. Regression test mirrors test_indexer_base_prepare_threshold_kei70st; negative-path verified. Post-merge: systemctl --user restart ceo-memory-indexer → Decisions count climbs off 300 within ~3min.
close_reason: already shipped — PR #1053 (atlas) merged 2026-05-18T23:12:47Z. ceo_memory_indexer.py line 160 now passes prepare_threshold=None per the bug spec. CI all green (SonarCloud, mypy, pytest, ruff). Empirical verify: cat scripts/orchestrator/ceo_memory_indexer.py | grep prepare_threshold.

title: Fix AgentMemories indexer 97% drift (192/7432)
description: elliot-memories-indexer.service active running but Weaviate AgentMemories=192 while Supabase public.agent_memories has 7432 non-archived rows (identity_fact=1600, decision=1357, lesson=997, milestone=978, pattern=713, ceo_instruction=385, daily_log=226...). Indexer emits zero log output in journalctl over last 30min. Likely: watermark logic broken, type filter too strict, or batch-fail silent-fail. Backfill remaining 7240 rows + add log emission on every batch (loud success/failure).
notes:
ROOT CAUSE (PR #1046): indexer_base.run_db_indexer missing prepare_threshold=None — Supabase pgbouncer txn-mode drops PREPARE between leases. psycopg3 auto-prepared after 5 executions, then DuplicatePreparedStatement → InvalidSqlStatementName loop for 20h. Pattern matches reference_psycopg_supabase_pgbouncer (KEI-54B PR #881); 12+ files in src/ already apply it.

DRIFT FRAMING CORRECTED:
- Spec said 192/7432 (97%) — two tables conflated
- elliot_internal.memories (what indexer reads): 1665 rows, 192 indexed → 88% drift, in-scope
- public.agent_memories: 7433 rows, 0 indexed → no indexer exists, OUT OF SCOPE for this fix

PR: https://github.com/Keiracom/Agency_OS/pull/1046
- One-line fix in indexer_base.py:262 (prepare_threshold=None)
- Regression test (negative-path verified)
- ruff clean
- Service restart deferred to post-merge (main worktree only; Atlas can't write to it)

FOLLOW-UP: Agency_OS-lsyd (P2) filed for public.agent_memories indexer + 7433-row backfill.

LOG ROUTING NOTE: original "no journalctl output" was diagnostic red herring — service routes to /home/elliotbot/clawd/logs/elliot-memories-indexer.log via StandardOutput=append:..., not journald. Log file has 30,961 lines of the error stream.

title: ENV schema validation hook — critical-5 env vars fail-fast at SessionStart
description:
## Problem

Misconfigured agent env (missing `DATABASE_URL`, `SLACK_BOT_TOKEN`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `CALLSIGN`) surfaces only when a downstream hook or the agent's first MCP call crashes. That's slow, obscure, and easy to miss when bringing up a new worktree on Vultr.

## Fix

`scripts/hooks/env_schema_validate.sh` checks the critical-5 at session start, prints a clear remediation message on miss, exits non-zero. Registered FIRST in the `SessionStart` `*` hook chain in `.claude/settings.json` so any miss surfaces immediately.

The hook auto-sources `$AGENCY_OS_ENV` (default `$HOME/.config/agency-os/.env`) when the calling shell hasn't already exported the vars — SessionStart contexts often start with a near-empty env.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `scripts/hooks/env_schema_validate.sh` exits non-zero when any of the 5 are missing, with a clear list naming them
- [ ] Empty-string env vars are treated as missing (catches botched rotations)
- [ ] `DATABASE_URL` OR `SUPABASE_DB_URL` satisfies the DB requirement
- [ ] Registered FIRST in `.claude/settings.json` `SessionStart` `*` chain (fail-fast)
- [ ] Bash syntax valid; `set -u` survives stripped-env callers (no HOME)
- [ ] 14 unit + integration tests pass

## Source

Dispatch from Elliot 2026-05-18 — STEP 0 PRE-CONFIRMED. Beads `Agency_OS-9erxio` P1.

title: KEI-222: CI ghost-green — pytest passes in CI but fails locally
description:
Dave directive 2026-05-18 + Max empirical finding: PR #1027 Backend Tests (Pytest) reported SUCCESS in CI (1m55s) but Max ran locally on origin/pull/1027/head and got verbatim:

AssertionError: assert 'aiden-relay refuses post to C0B2PM3TV0B' in 'R_CEO_FORMAT_BLOCKED: #ceo post violates plain-English bullets-only convention...'

Aiden independently reproduced. CI false-green is systemic — every PR we've merged today on green pytest signal is suspect.

Investigation scope:
- CI workflow cached from previous green commit? Check workflow run logs for PR #1027.
- Test collection excludes the file? Check pytest.ini / pyproject.toml / conftest filters.
- CI runs different test set than local pytest? Compare CI command vs local invocation.
- Workflow filter excluding this branch? Check .github/workflows/*.yml for path/branch filters.

Verification: run a controlled experiment — submit a PR with a deliberately failing test, confirm CI marks RED. Then audit recent PRs merged today (#1026, #1023, #1022, #1021, #1020) for similar ghost-green risk.
acceptance_criteria: Root cause identified + fix in place that makes deliberately failing tests show RED in CI

title: [WORKER] Drive → Weaviate indexer — StrategicDocuments collection + bulk backfill + incremental sync
description:
## Why

Drive docs contain all ratified strategic decisions — roadmap, team structure, deliberation floor spec, architecture. Agents cannot semantically query Drive. Everything in Drive needs to be in Weaviate so agents can retrieve context without re-explaining it every session.

## What

Build a Drive → Weaviate indexer. Same pattern as [KEI-201](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-201/scout-slack-historical-extraction-to-weaviate-bulk-backfill) (Slack → Weaviate).

**Phase 1 — Create StrategicDocuments collection in Weaviate**

```python
{
  "class": "StrategicDocuments",
  "vectorizer": "text2vec-openai",
  "properties": [
    {"name": "doc_id", "dataType": ["text"]},
    {"name": "doc_url", "dataType": ["text"]},
    {"name": "title", "dataType": ["text"]},
    {"name": "section", "dataType": ["text"]},
    {"name": "content", "dataType": ["text"]},
    {"name": "updated_at", "dataType": ["date"]},
    {"name": "ratified_by", "dataType": ["text"]},
    {"name": "ratified_at", "dataType": ["date"]}
  ]
}
```

**Phase 2 — Bulk backfill these docs immediately**

```
1Br6SsCKizvyNk6dOvm9EQE_8drut1WRId7WJNddilDA  Roadmap (ratified 2026-05-18)
1U5uet-IxJSNhE-iWlVdW3eZpNR8obf-7j3BzvaEWdH4  Deliberation Floor Flow Architecture v2
113Ej0n62uS_qmyMwvase54kFI1eCCwHHwwrMN9GDlCc  Team Structure (ratified 2026-05-18)
1NNoA-6MgXZS6mk35QCeer3mmEnqNVRSD7NBmu0fzfHw  Architecture FINAL (Parts 1-16)
1kf-MVHeHaViwlMuy3ti_pZCS1DJE9ox8vIa3aZdspkY  Team Structure Brief v2
1Br6SsCKizvyNk6dOvm9EQE_8drut1WRId7WJNddilDA  Official product roadmap
```

Chunk strategy: split by heading (h1/h2/h3 boundaries), not fixed token size. Each section becomes one Weaviate object. Preserves semantic coherence.

**Phase 3 — Incremental indexer**

* Poll Drive API every 6 hours for modifiedTime changes on target docs
* On change: re-fetch, re-chunk, upsert (overwrite by doc_id + section)
* Add new docs by appending their ID to a config file: `config/drive_index_targets.json`
* No hardcoded doc IDs in the indexer code — all driven by config

**Phase 4 — Validation**
Run semantic query: "what did Dave decide about team structure" 
Confirm top result returns from StrategicDocuments collection with certainty > 0.70
Paste verbatim result.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] StrategicDocuments collection exists in Weaviate with OpenAI vectorizer
- [ ] All 5 target docs chunked and indexed (row count per doc posted)
- [ ] Incremental indexer running as systemd service or cron
- [ ] Semantic validation query returns relevant hit > 0.70 certainty
- [ ] drive_index_targets.json config file exists for adding new docs
- [ ] ceo_memory updated: ceo:memory:drive_indexer = live

title: [WORKER] GitHub PR merge webhook → auto-close Supabase task
description:
## Problem

PR merges on GitHub don't update Supabase task status. KEI tasks stay "available" or "active" after their PR merges. This breaks the dependency chain — Phase N+1 tasks don't surface because Phase N tasks never show as "done".

This is structural staleness. NATS doesn't fix it. The Dispatcher + webhook bridge does.

## Solution

Extend the existing GitHub webhook handler to close tasks on PR merge.

When a PR merge event fires:

1. Extract the PR title and body
2. Parse KEI ID from title (regex: `KEI-\d+`)
3. Fallback: match via `linear_url` column if title parse fails
4. Run: `UPDATE tasks SET status='done', updated_at=NOW() WHERE id = 'KEI-XXX'`
5. Dispatcher picks up via Supabase Realtime — next phase tasks surface automatically

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] PR merge webhook event handled in existing webhook handler
- [ ] KEI ID parsed from PR title (regex: `KEI-\d+`)
- [ ] Fallback: match via `linear_url` column if title parse fails
- [ ] Task marked `done` in Supabase on merge only (not on open/push)
- [ ] Test: merge a test PR → confirm task status updates within 5 seconds
- [ ] Paste verbatim Supabase row showing status='done' after test merge
- [ ] If no matching KEI found: log and skip, do not error

title: [ELLIOT] Supervisor dep-blocked drift fix — regex parse 'FOLLOW-UP after KEI-N' before claim dispatch
description:
Aiden flagged 3rd drift category. Mechanical fix via regex parse on KEI title + description for dep-blocked work.

**Context:** 3-way ratified (Aiden + Max + Elliot). Path (b) regex over path (a) JSONB column. Reasons: ships in 1 session (\~5-10 LoC at claim_next_task SELECT), empirically covers all 4 drift patterns this session.

**Patterns to match:**

* `FOLLOW-UP after KEI-N` (e.g. KEI-191)
* `sub of KEI-N` (e.g. KEI-193/194)
* `(KEI-N follow-up)` (e.g. KEI-196/197/198)
* `depends on KEI-N` (Linear convention)

**Implementation:** in scripts/fleet_supervisor.py claim_next_task, after fetching candidate task, extract blocker KEI-IDs from title + description via regex. Query: `SELECT id FROM public.tasks WHERE id IN (...blockers...) AND status != 'done'`. If non-empty result: skip this candidate, try next.

**Acceptance:** test KEI 'FOLLOW-UP after KEI-185' exists; KEI-185 status='active' (not done); supervisor cycle does NOT dispatch the FOLLOW-UP KEI; logs 'skip — blocker KEI-185 not done'.

**Sub-KEI under KEI-183** (Supervisor v2). Sister to KEI-194 (JWT hash rotation) + KEI-199 (PR-existence pre-check). Three sub-KEIs close all 3 supervisor blind spots: already-shipped, cross-lane, dep-blocked.

**Long-term (a) JSONB path** filed separately as future follow-up after persona-column lessons (KEI-183) prove out the Linear-webhook-writer pattern.

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1 **Granularity:** 1 session. 3-way ratified (Max + Aiden + Elliot).

title: [SCOUT] Slack historical extraction to Weaviate — bulk backfill + incremental indexer
description:
## Why

Slack contains the complete operational history of the Keiracom system — months of fleet supervisor observations, CEO directives, agent escalations, architectural decisions, completion reports, and debugging sessions. None of this is currently indexed in Weaviate. Extracting it would dramatically enrich the memory system with historical patterns that agents can immediately draw on.

## What

Bulk historical extraction of all relevant Slack channels into Weaviate, then ongoing incremental indexing.

**Channels to extract:**

* #ceo — CEO directives, fleet status, escalations, architectural decisions
* #execution — Agent work, CONCUR events, PR reviews, completions
* #completed_directives — Completion records
* #alerts — System alerts and incidents
* #ops — Operational events

**What NOT to extract:**

* \[READY:callsign\] heartbeat spam — pure noise, no signal value
* Fleet status posts (fleet supervisor will write these directly to agent_memories via [KEI-200](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-200/atlas-fleet-supervisor-writes-observations-to-agent-memories-on-every) going forward)
* Duplicate messages (dedup by content hash)

**Categorisation on ingest:**
Each message gets tagged by type before indexing:

* `ceo_directive` — CEO instructions and decisions
* `agent_escalation` — Agent asking for help or flagging a blocker
* `architectural_decision` — Design or architecture ratification
* `supervisor_observation` — Fleet supervisor detection + action
* `completion_report` — Task completion with evidence
* `debug_session` — Problem diagnosis and resolution
* `governance_event` — CONCUR, HOLD, ratification events

**Target Weaviate collection:** `slack_history` (new collection, tagged by channel + type + timestamp)

## Implementation

1. Use Slack API to paginate through each channel's history from the beginning
2. Filter out noise messages (\[READY\], basic fleet status heartbeats)
3. Categorise each message using a lightweight classifier
4. Write to Weaviate `slack_history` collection with text2vec-transformers vectorizer
5. Store metadata: channel, timestamp, callsign/author, message_type, thread_ts if threaded
6. After bulk load: set up incremental indexer (runs every 15 minutes, indexes new messages since last run)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `slack_history` Weaviate collection created with text2vec-transformers vectorizer
- [ ] Historical messages extracted from all 5 channels with noise filtered
- [ ] Messages categorised by type
- [ ] Semantic query "psycopg asyncpg DSN bug" returns relevant results from historical #execution messages
- [ ] Semantic query "fleet supervisor idle detection" returns relevant results
- [ ] Incremental indexer running as systemd service, updating every 15 minutes

## Value

This gives the memory system immediate access to the complete operational history of the system. Every problem ever encountered, every solution ever applied, every decision ever made — all immediately retrievable semantically. Agents stop repeating mistakes that are documented in Slack history.
notes: Shipped via PR #1044 (commit d9c613390). 5/5 spec channels live in Slack_history (14,772 rows). Root cause: resolve_channel_ids defaulted to public_channel only — silently dropped private channels. Fix: types=public_channel,private_channel + exclude_archived=False. Together with PR #1001 + PR #1025, KEI-201 fully delivered. GOV-9 GAP (79.5% agent_chatter fallback) standing as separate KEI.

title: [ATLAS] Fleet supervisor writes observations to agent_memories on every detection and resolution
description:
## Why

The fleet supervisor detects \~25 types of agent idle scenarios every cycle and takes corrective action. Currently all observations go only to Slack and are lost. This institutional knowledge — what broke, when, why, how it was resolved — is exactly what Weaviate needs to eventually replace the supervisor with intelligent Dispatcher routing.

## What

Add a write call to `agent_memories` on every supervisor cycle where a detection + resolution occurs.

**Write on each of these events:**

* Stale claim detected and released
* Dead session detected and restarted
* Agent idle with queue items — task injected
* PR re-dispatched (and to which agent)
* Cross-lane assignment made (and why)
* Agent context full — restarted
* Any recovery action taken

**Schema for each write:**

```python
{
  "callsign": "system",
  "source_type": "supervisor_observation",
  "content": "{agent} was {idle_reason} at {timestamp}. Action taken: {action}. Result: {result}",
  "typed_metadata": {
    "agent": "<callsign>",
    "idle_reason": "<stale_claim|dead_session|no_claim_queue_items|context_full|...>",
    "action_taken": "<released|restarted|injected|re-dispatched>",
    "task_id": "<kei_id if applicable>",
    "resolved": true/false
  },
  "tags": ["supervisor", "fleet_observation", "<idle_reason>"],
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "trust": 1.0
}
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Supervisor writes one `agent_memories` row per detection+resolution event per cycle
- [ ] Rows visible in Supabase with callsign=system, source_type=supervisor_observation
- [ ] After 3 supervisor cycles, at least one observation row exists per agent
- [ ] Weaviate indexer picks these up on next index cycle (via AgentMemories collection)

## Why this matters

This is the dataset that allows the Dispatcher + Weaviate to eventually replace the supervisor. Every pattern it learns from resolving idle scenarios becomes retrievable institutional knowledge. The supervisor builds its own replacement.

title: [ELLIOT] Supervisor PR-existence pre-check — skip claim-dispatch if open PR mentions KEI (4 drift-syncs anchor)
description:
Aiden flagged 4 drift-syncs this session: KEI-90, KEI-122, KEI-187, KEI-188 — each got auto-claimed by the supervisor AFTER an OPEN PR existed implementing the work. Agents had to drift-sync done state + write task_verifications by hand.

**Root cause:** supervisor's claim_next_task query checks `WHERE status='available'` but doesn't query GitHub for an OPEN PR whose title or body mentions the KEI.

**Fix:** Add `pr_exists_for_kei(kei_id)` helper in fleet_supervisor.py mirroring the existing `agent_has_reviewed` shape. Before claim dispatch, call it:

```python
if pr_exists_for_kei(kei_id):
    log.info(f"[supervisor] skip {kei_id} — open PR already in flight")
    continue
```

Implementation: `gh pr list --state open --search "<KEI-ID>" --json number,title,body` then bool(len(results) > 0). Cache results within a single supervisor cycle to avoid redundant gh calls.

**Sub-KEI under KEI-183** (Supervisor v2). Can ship in parallel or as fix-up after KEI-183 merges.

**Acceptance:** test PR #N exists with title containing 'KEI-NN'; supervisor cycle queries KEI-NN; supervisor logs 'skip — open PR' and does NOT inject claim or send tmux prompt.

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1 — mechanical drift prevention, anchored on 4 real drift-syncs this session.

title: M3: Update agent_query default min_score to score-distribution-aware (KEI-192 follow-up)
description:
Aiden's audit revealed hard min_score=0.50 floor is fragile. When scores are all 0.0 (vectorizer regression), everything filters out. Defensive: replace hard floor with p50 of returned scores OR top-N picker that returns best-available regardless of score.

**Fix:** edit src/retrieval/agent_query.py — instead of `qualified = (c for c in citations if c.score >= min_score)`, use `top_n = sorted(citations, key=lambda c: c.score, reverse=True)[:k]` — always return best-N regardless of absolute scores. Add hard fallback OFF only when ALL scores are zero (sentinel flag).

**Acceptance:** test with synthetic 0-score citations returns top-N anyway (not empty); test with mixed-score citations returns highest-scored first; integration test against current vectorless collections returns SOMETHING not nothing.

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1 — defensive against future vectorizer regressions. Lower than M1 but still important.

title: Install 4 indexers as systemd user services on this host (was KEI-th6 in Beads)
description:
Per Dave 2026-05-18 execution directive Step 1-7 parallel slot for \[ATLAS\].

The 4 indexers exist as code + systemd unit files but NOT installed as user services. This is the load-bearing memory-pipeline gap (per Aiden + Max in 7-area deliberation).

**The 4 indexers:**

* linear_state_indexer (scripts/orchestrator/linear_state_indexer.py + infra/systemd/agents/linear-state-indexer.service + scripts/install_linear_state_indexer.sh)
* git_commits_indexer (same shape, KEI-85 phase C)
* ceo_memory_indexer (already running per earlier session — verify)
* elliot_memories_indexer (KEI-109 sub 2/3 — verify)

**Lane clarification for Atlas:** No Vultr SSH needed. The agent worktree IS on the Vultr host (you ARE on Vultr; /home/elliotbot/clawd/Agency_OS-atlas is local). The install scripts are `bash scripts/install_<X>.sh` running as elliotbot user; they use `systemctl --user enable + start` not system-level.

**Acceptance:**

* `systemctl --user is-active linear-state-indexer = active`
* `systemctl --user is-active git-commits-indexer = active`
* `systemctl --user is-active ceo-memory-indexer = active` (or verify existing systemd unit)
* `systemctl --user is-active elliot-memories-indexer = active`
* All 4 timers firing on their scheduled cadence
* Weaviate Aggregate counts on Keis + Codebase + Decisions + AgentMemories increase within first poll cycle

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1 **Granularity:** 1 session. Per 3-way ratified consolidated sequence step 4.

Without short-TTL + auto-reissue, the day Dave ratifies a new global_governance_patterns row = every active container halts mid-task on next Dispatcher call. Implement per Max's path (a): JWT TTL 5min + auth_minter auto-reissue on hash mismatch (matches KEI-115C existing 1-hour JWT expiry pattern). **Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1 **Granularity:** 1 session. Refinement of KEI-185 scope. 3-way ratified.

title: KEI-H: Supervisor symmetric HOLD parsing — recognize [REVIEW:HOLD:callsign] same as APPROVE

Add HOLD pattern to fleet_supervisor.agent_has_reviewed: parse \[REVIEW:HOLD:callsign\] + \[REVIEW:HOLD-FINAL:callsign\] same as approve markers; if any HOLD by callsign present, skip dispatching.

**Acceptance:** supervisor runs cycle, PR has \[REVIEW:HOLD:scout\] comment, supervisor does NOT inject review prompt to scout; agent confirms via tmux capture no auto-claim prompt.

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1 **Granularity:** 1 session. 3-way ratified (Aiden addition).

title: LiteLLM Prisma can't connect to Supabase pooler — connection layer fix
description:
KEI-178 follow-up. The Anthropic-credit-block was resolved (boot check now Gemini-first per Dave directive) but a deeper layer remains:

LiteLLM's Prisma client fails on startup with httpx.ConnectError 'All connection attempts failed' when trying to connect to Supabase pooler ([aws-1-ap-southeast-1.pooler.supabase.com:6543](<http://aws-1-ap-southeast-1.pooler.supabase.com:6543>)).

Suspected causes (any/all):

* Password URL-encoding (current password contains '!' which may need %21)
* Pooler requires SSL/TLS that Prisma isn't configuring
* Pooler txn mode incompatible with Prisma's prepared-statement pattern (same family as reference_psycopg_supabase_pgbouncer pin — psycopg needs prepare_threshold=None)
* Network firewall on outbound to pooler port

Progress so far (commit-ready, just not pushed):

* /home/elliotbot/clawd/Agency_OS/scripts/litellm_boot_check.py — Gemini-first logic (Anthropic optional)
* /home/elliotbot/.config/litellm/config.yaml — Gemini 2.5 models
* /home/elliotbot/.config/agency-os/.env — GOOGLE_API_KEY alias added
* /home/elliotbot/.config/systemd/user/litellm.service.d/no-prisma.conf — DATABASE_URL override stripping +asyncpg
* prisma client generated in litellm-venv (prisma-client-py shebang fix + prisma generate run successfully)

**Acceptance:** systemctl --user is-active litellm = active (not activating); curl [http://127.0.0.1:4000/health](<http://127.0.0.1:4000/health>) returns 200.

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1

title: Fix completion_sync_worker drive_manual sink — write_manual_mirror.py rejects --task-id flag
description:
67 rows stuck in completion_sync_queue at attempts=3 with: write_manual_mirror.py: error: unrecognized arguments: --task-id KEI-NNN

The worker code (src/orchestrator/completion_sync_worker.py or similar) passes --task-id <KEI> to write_manual_mirror.py but the script doesn't accept that flag.

Fix: either (a) add --task-id arg support to scripts/write_manual_mirror.py, or (b) remove the --task-id flag from the worker's drive_manual sink call.

After fix: reset attempts=0 on the 67 stuck drive_manual rows so they re-try.

**Acceptance:** completion_sync_queue rows with target_sink=drive_manual process successfully; queue depth drops below 5.

title: Slack indexer (KEI-109 sub-pipeline 3/3) — Slack messages → Weaviate via conversations.history
description:
Final sub-pipeline of KEI-109. ceo_memory_indexer (existing) + elliot_memories_indexer (PR #940 commit 6f0da690) shipped. Slack indexer remaining.

Aiden + Atlas concurred on option (a): direct Slack Web API conversations.history poll. Rationale: (i) mirrors git_commits cursor pattern (channel_id + ts cursor), (ii) no new Supabase schema = no migration coordination = ships in one PR, (iii) Slack-API rate limits are well-documented per-channel and the 429-Retry-After helper extracted via PR #937 can be reused.

Acceptance:
- scripts/orchestrator/slack_indexer.py reads conversations.history per relevant channel (#ceo, #execution, #completed_directives) using channel_id + ts cursor.
- Deterministic UUID via uuid5("slack", f"{channel_id}:{ts}").
- Filters bot/system messages if needed; agent messages get agent property derived from sender or message tag.
- Indexes into a Weaviate class (proposed: SlackMessages with 5 mandatory BaseIndexer ABC properties).
- infra/systemd/agents/slack-indexer.service + scripts/install_slack_indexer.sh + tests/scripts/test_slack_indexer.py.
- KEI-108 anti-false-complete gate satisfied.
- Live --once smoke: outcome with success > 0, failed = 0.
- Can use indexer_base.run_db_indexer pattern as parent OR a new run_http_indexer sibling — note in Agency_OS-qdo's review that 2 of 3 existing indexers (linear, git) are also non-psycopg-backed and may share this run_http_indexer helper.

Parent KEI-109. Atlas filed this split to release the KEI-109 claim for KEI-103 work. Unassigned — first-come-first-served per Dave standing order.
notes: Filed by atlas as split of KEI-109. Slack source decision: option (a) — direct conversations.history (Aiden + Atlas concur). SLACK_BOT_TOKEN is in env per slack_relay.py reference.

description: Build indexer pipelines for: (1) Slack messages (#ceo, #execution) → Weaviate, (2) ceo_memory rows → Weaviate, (3) elliot_internal.memories → Weaviate. These feed the collective intelligence layer so agents can recall what was discussed, decided, and discovered across all channels. title: KEI-87 follow-up — migrate ~11 ceo:* writer call-sites to ceo_memory_writer wrapper
description:
## Why

KEI-87 (PR #922 / commit ac0150ad) shipped the mechanical write-guard trigger for ceo_memory keys with `ceo:*` prefix. Migration is NOT auto-applied — Max's banner says: caller-migrate → apply → tighten. Without this caller migration, applying the trigger via `mcp__supabase__apply_migration` BREAKS all existing ceo:\* writers (they don't `SET LOCAL agency_os.callsign`).

## Audit (Aiden 2026-05-17 — deep-check complete)

*IN SCOPE — 11 ceo:* writer call-sites requiring wrapper migration:\*

 1. `scripts/gmb_pilot2.py:617` + `:620` — `key='ceo:directives'`
 2. `scripts/gmb_pilot2_resume.py:435` + `:438` — `key='ceo:directives'`
 3. `scripts/gmb_pilot2_retry.py:450` + `:453` — `key='ceo:directives'`
 4. `scripts/gmb_pilot3.py:796` + `:799` — `key='ceo:directives'`
 5. `scripts/gmb_pilot3_resume.py:607` + `:610` — `key='ceo:directives'`
 6. `scripts/gmb_pilot4.py:652` + `:655` + `:657` — `key='ceo:directives'`
 7. `scripts/gmb_process_snapshot_v2.py:277` + `:282` — `key='ceo:directives'`
 8. `scripts/gmb_process_snapshot.py:175` — `key='ceo:directive_225_complete'` (deep-check confirmed)
 9. `scripts/session_end_hook.py:197` — `key=f'ceo:session_end_<date>_<callsign>'`
10. `src/services/cis_outcome_service.py:384` — `key='ceo:propensity_weights_v3'` (deep-check confirmed)
11. `scripts/three_store_save.py:224` — `key=f'ceo:directive_{directive}_complete'` (deep-check confirmed)

*OUT OF SCOPE — trigger ignores (non-ceo:* prefix):\*

* `src/observability/heartbeat.py:130` — `key='heartbeat:<service>'`
* `scripts/orchestrator/completion_sync_worker.py:95` — `key=f'completion:{task_id}'` (deep-check confirmed)

Audit closed. 11 confirmed in-scope (matches Max's \~11 estimate from migration banner). 0 remaining uncertain.

## Spec

Migrate each `ceo:*` writer to `from src.governance.ceo_memory_writer import upsert_ceo_memory_key, update_ceo_memory_value` (canonical wrapper from KEI-87 / PR #922). The wrapper:

* accepts callsign explicitly (from `CALLSIGN` env or arg)
* runs `BEGIN; SET LOCAL agency_os.callsign = '<callsign>'; UPDATE/INSERT; COMMIT`
* handles psycopg connection with `prepare_threshold=None` (Supabase pgbouncer txn-mode)

For the gmb_pilot\* writers (all write `ceo:directives` last-number tracking), the wrapper call is:

```python
upsert_ceo_memory_key(os.environ.get('CALLSIGN', 'unknown'), 'ceo:directives', {...})
```

Note: wrapper currently does full value replacement. The gmb_pilot\* writers use `jsonb_set` to patch a single field — needs either a JSONB-patch helper added to the wrapper OR caller-side read-modify-write semantics.

three_store_save.py + cis_outcome_service.py use async (asyncpg / SQLAlchemy text). Wrapper currently uses sync psycopg — async caller-side either uses asyncio.to_thread OR an async wrapper variant needs to be added.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 11 confirmed ceo:\* writer call-sites migrated to wrapper.
- [ ] gmb_pilot jsonb_set sites: either wrapper grows a JSONB-patch helper OR call-site does read-modify-write.
- [ ] Async call-sites (three_store_save, cis_outcome_service): either asyncio.to_thread bridge OR async wrapper variant.
- [ ] Each call-site passes correct callsign source (CALLSIGN env or hardcoded if context-bound).
- [ ] Tests updated/added: existing writer tests still pass; new tests assert wrapper invocation (mock psycopg).
- [ ] Negative test: synthetic UPDATE without SET LOCAL raises `psycopg.errors.CheckViolation` post-migration.
- [ ] KEI-108 anti-false-complete gate PASS (no new .service or webhook).
- [ ] After this PR merges + CI green, Max applies the KEI-87 trigger migration via `mcp__supabase__apply_migration`. Verbatim post-apply: synthetic UPDATE with `agency_os.callsign='aiden'` on ceo:\* key returns CheckViolation; same UPDATE with elliot succeeds.

## Sequencing

1. THIS KEI: migrate call-sites (this PR). Phase 0.5.
2. THEN: Max applies migration (Supabase MCP). Phase 0.5.
3. KEI-87 ships fully (trigger + caller compliance).

## Owner

Aiden (audit lead). Pair with Orion for the call-site rewriting if KEI-89 Gate 2 merge stalls; otherwise serial after KEI-89.

## Source

Aiden audit + deep-check during KEI-89 Gate 2 build downtime (2026-05-17). 3-way concur cleared on the audit propose; deep-check cleared by Max ts \~1779021850.

title: Cross-cutting ceo_memory write-guard (GOV-12 mechanical) — covers 400+ keys
description:
## What

Aiden's audit (during PR #917 review) found ALL 400+ rows in public.ceo_memory share the same write-guard hole: no RLS, no trigger, no code-level guard. Any callsign with DATABASE_URL can UPDATE any "supposedly CEO-only" key. The `ceo:` prefix is documentation-hint only.

This is a GOV-12 violation (Gates As Code, Not Comments). KEI-86 (Max's phase-lock) added READ enforcement but the WRITE side inherits the cross-cutting gap.

## Pattern-fix vs point-fix

Patching one key per PR (KEI-86 phase-lock, etc) is point-fix and leaves 400+ keys unguarded. Single cross-cutting guard closes the entire surface; subsequent PRs inherit it for free.

## Spec

Pick one of:

(a) Supabase RLS policy on public.ceo_memory restricting UPDATE/INSERT on keys matching prefix `ceo:` (or a configurable allowlist) to a specific role / app user.

(b) BEFORE UPDATE/INSERT trigger that checks `current_setting('agency_os.callsign', true)` matches a configurable allow-list (elliot, dave) and raises EXCEPTION otherwise. Trigger fires per row.

Either path: requires plumbing `SET LOCAL agency_os.callsign = 'elliot'` (or equivalent) into the writer code so the trigger sees the caller identity. That's the meaningful change — every existing writer (slack_relay, openclaw_auto_kei, etc) gets a small wrapper that sets the session var before issuing the UPDATE.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Migration adds RLS or trigger covering all `ceo:` prefix keys (or allowlist).
- [ ] Writer wrapper installed in all known ceo_memory call-sites (grep src/ scripts/ for `UPDATE public.ceo_memory` / `INSERT INTO public.ceo_memory` and audit).
- [ ] Negative-path test: synthetic UPDATE with `agency_os.callsign='aiden'` on a `ceo:*` key returns permission denied / EXCEPTION (paste verbatim).
- [ ] Positive-path test: same UPDATE with `agency_os.callsign='elliot'` succeeds (paste verbatim).
- [ ] KEI-108 anti-false-complete gate PASS.

## Why not point-fix on KEI-86

Per Max's reasoning + Aiden's audit: 400+ keys share the same gap. Patching only KEI-86's phase-lock is theatre — any caller can still mutate every other ceo: key. Cross-cutting fix is the structural answer.

## Source

Aiden's audit during PR #917 review, ratified by Max + Elliot. Surfaced to Dave alongside PR #917 option (c) sign-off request.

title: KEI-68 — Living governance layer: rules in Supabase, not static markdown
description:
## Problem

Governance lives in static markdown files — CLAUDE.md, GOVERNANCE.md, IDENTITY.md, Enforcer rules. When architecture changes, governance must be manually updated across 6 agent worktrees. It often doesn't catch up. Agents operate under stale rules.

Tonight alone, six governance changes were implied by architectural decisions:

* \[READY:callsign\] pattern deprecated → Enforcer rules watching for it need updating
* Beads replaced by Supabase → rules about `bd linear sync --pull` become obsolete
* Prime-clone pairings ratified → rules about who posts to #execution need updating
* Deliberation gate designed → `deliberation_required: true` classification needs governance
* bd complete hook shipped → polling fallback rules change
* tasks table live → claiming rules change

Each requires manually editing files across 6 worktrees. Each is a gap where agents operate under outdated rules until someone notices.

## Fix: Governance as structured data in Supabase

A `governance_rules` table — rules as rows, not markdown. Agents query on session start. Rules are always current.

```sql
CREATE TABLE governance_rules (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  category TEXT NOT NULL,  -- 'communication', 'claiming', 'concur', 'deliberation'
  rule TEXT NOT NULL,
  active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
  deprecated_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  deprecated_reason TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

When architecture changes, rule changes in the same PR — not separately, not later. Deprecated rules are flagged with reason, not deleted. Agents query `WHERE active = true`. History is preserved.

## Enforcer evolution

Enforcer patterns come from the governance table, not hardcoded regex. When \[READY:callsign\] is deprecated, one UPDATE to the table. Enforcer regex updates automatically on next session start. No CLAUDE.md edits across 6 worktrees.

## Session start protocol update

CEO and agents query governance_rules on session start alongside tasks table. Always current. Never stale.

## Acceptance criteria

* governance_rules table created with initial ruleset migrated from [CLAUDE.md/GOVERNANCE.md](<http://CLAUDE.md/GOVERNANCE.md>)
* Agents query table on session start (not read markdown files)
* Enforcer patterns sourced from table
* When a rule changes: one SQL UPDATE, no markdown edits required
* Deprecated rules preserved with reason and timestamp

## Priority

High — not urgent. Ships after [KEI-22](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-22/kei-22-automated-linear-beads-bidirectional-sync) and [KEI-45](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-45/kei-45-event-driven-pull-model-supabase-realtime-subscriptions) complete. Governance stability matters but doesn't block the idle fix.

title: KEI-52 — Claude Code tool call log export: capture what agents actually did into Weaviate
description:
## What

At each session compaction or completion, export the Claude Code tool call log as a structured record and index it into Weaviate. Captures what the agent actually did — files read, commands run, errors encountered — without noise.

## Why

The tmux pane contains "Generating... thinking some more" noise. The tool call log inside Claude Code contains the signal: every file read, every bash command run, every error hit, every file written. This is what the agent actually experienced, not what they were thinking about.

## What gets captured

```
Tool call: Read file → /home/elliotbot/clawd/Agency_OS/src/cognee/client.py
Tool call: Bash → cat /proc/meminfo | grep MemAvailable
  Output: MemAvailable: 892044 kB
Tool call: Bash → ulimit -v 3145728 && python cognee_ingest.py
  Output: Error: MemoryError — virtual memory limit exceeded
Tool call: Bash → python cognee_ingest.py --cgroup-limit 3g
  Output: Stream 3 starting: 1289 chunks...
```

Not the thinking about what to try. The actual tries and their results.

## What gets indexed

Structured JSON per session:

```json
{
  "session_id": "max-2026-05-13-cognee",
  "agent": "max",
  "kei": "KEI-44",
  "duration_hours": 4.2,
  "files_read": ["src/cognee/client.py", "scripts/cognee_ingest.py"],
  "commands_run": [{"cmd": "ulimit -v...", "outcome": "failed"}, ...],
  "errors_encountered": ["MemoryError", "Lance concurrent write"],
  "files_written": ["scripts/run_cognee.sh"]
}
```

## Export mechanism

* Claude Code stores conversation history including all tool uses
* At compaction trigger: export tool calls from current context
* Parse into structured format above
* Index into Weaviate `sessions` collection

## What this enables

Agent B queries: "what commands were tried for the Cognee memory problem?"
Gets: every command Max ran, with outcomes. Knows instantly which approaches failed.

## Acceptance criteria

* Tool call log exported at every compaction and session end
* Structured JSON format consistently applied
* Indexed into Weaviate sessions collection
* Test query: "what bash commands were run for [KEI-44](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-44/kei-44-cognee-subprocess-3gb-memory-cap-cgroupulimit)?" returns Max's command history

## Dependencies

[KEI-46](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-46/kei-45-layer-3-idle-enforcement-daemon-systemd-polling-service-per) + [KEI-47](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-47/kei-migration-completeness-guard-regex-false-positive-on-update-from)

## Owner

Scout — after [KEI-47](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-47/kei-migration-completeness-guard-regex-false-positive-on-update-from) ships.

title: KEI — Migration Completeness Guard regex false-positive on "UPDATE FROM" prose
description:
## Problem

`.github/workflows/ci.yml` Migration Completeness Guard uses this regex on removed diff lines:

```
(INSERT[[:space:]]+INTO|UPDATE)[[:space:]]+[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_.]*
```

`awk '{print $NF}'` then takes the last field, which means a prose pattern like `[UPDATE FROM {callsign}]` gets parsed as `UPDATE FROM` and the script tries to verify residual readers of a "table" called `FROM` — a false positive.

## Manifested in

PR #843 (Aiden, KEI-41 Phase 2 Telegram rename) — guard failed because a deleted f-string `[UPDATE FROM {callsign}]` got captured. No actual schema change in the PR.

## Fix options

1. Exclude SQL reserved words from valid target list (FROM, WHERE, SET, INTO, JOIN, AS, WHERE, etc).
2. Use a stricter pattern: require the target to look like a real table identifier (schema.table or just lowercase identifiers, exclude all-caps SQL keywords).
3. Run the check only on changes inside files known to contain SQL (migrations/, .sql files, anything with `sqlalchemy` or `asyncpg` imports).

## Acceptance criteria

* PR #843-equivalent case passes the guard.
* A real removed-INSERT-without-migration still fails.
* New unit test in `tests/scripts/test_check_migration_completeness.py` covering the prose false-positive case.

## Ownership

Open — Atlas or Orion when one of them clears their current PR. Not blocking PR #843 merge (Dave override path: confirm no schema change in the diff and merge with admin).
