title: Fleet Supervisor — mechanical monitor + auto-resolve for all 6 agents (replaces manual tmux checking)
description:
## What

Fully mechanical fleet supervisor that replaces manual tmux monitoring. Runs every 5 minutes via systemd timer. Diagnoses AND resolves every idle scenario — no human or agent decision point.

## Architecture

`scripts/fleet_supervisor.py` + `fleet-supervisor.timer` (systemd)

For each of 6 agents, every 5 minutes:

### Scenario 1: No claim + queue has items

* Supabase: `SELECT id FROM tasks WHERE status='available' ORDER BY priority LIMIT 1 FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`
* Supabase: `UPDATE tasks SET status='active', claimed_by=$CALLSIGN`
* Linear: fetch KEI description
* tmux: `send-keys` inject full task description into agent stdin
* Result: agent receives work mechanically

### Scenario 2: No claim + queue empty

* GitHub API: check for open PRs needing review (where agent is not author, not yet reviewed)
* If found: INSERT review KEI into tasks + claim for this agent + inject
* If none: agent is correctly idle, log "queue empty"

### Scenario 3: Claimed + no tool_call_log entry in 15 minutes

* Query agent context usage (parse tmux capture for context indicator)
* If 100%: `systemctl --user restart {agent}.service` + git pull + cognee inject + re-claim same KEI + inject
* If <100%: tmux send-keys nudge: "You have KEI-{id} claimed. Resume building. Task: {title}"

### Scenario 4: tmux session dead

* `tmux has-session -t {callsign}` returns non-zero
* `systemctl --user restart {agent}.service`
* Wait 10s for boot
* Claim next available + inject

### Scenario 5: Agent shipped PR + went idle (no claim, has open PR)

* GitHub API: agent has open PR with no reviews yet
* Create review KEI for PR (excludes author)
* Claim next BUILD KEI for idle agent
* Agent starts next task instead of waiting for review

### Scenario 6: Stale claims (active > 2 hours, no task_verification)

* `UPDATE tasks SET status='available', claimed_by=NULL WHERE status='active' AND updated_at < NOW() - interval '2 hours' AND id NOT IN (SELECT task_id FROM task_verifications)`
* Released items become claimable next cycle

## Output

One #ceo Slack message every 5 minutes:

```
Fleet Status [20:30 UTC]
atlas: building KEI-166 (last tool call 2m ago) ✅
aiden: building KEI-114 (last tool call 45s ago) ✅
orion: was idle → claimed KEI-105, injected task ⚡
scout: was idle → claimed KEI-155, injected task ⚡
elliot: 100% context → restarted, re-claimed KEI-151 🔄
max: shipped PR #957, idle → claimed KEI-104 ⚡
Queue: 28 available | 6 active | 65 done
```

## Acceptance

1. systemd timer fires every 5 minutes — verify with `systemctl list-timers`
2. Script detects idle agent + claims + injects — verify with Supabase claimed_by appearing without manual intervention
3. Script detects 100% context + restarts + re-claims — verify with service restart log + new claim in Supabase
4. Script detects dead tmux + restarts — verify by killing a tmux session and watching recovery
5. Script posts to #ceo — verify message appears with correct per-agent status
6. All 6 scenarios tested empirically, not just "it should work"

## Dependencies

* Supabase tasks table (exists)
* tool_call_log table (exists)
* tmux sessions (exist)
* GitHub API access (exists via gh CLI)
* Slack webhook (exists)
* systemd user services (exist)

## Phase: 0 Priority: P0 — this is the single highest-leverage infrastructure piece. It makes the fleet self-healing.

title: KEEPALIVE_PULL_MODE conservative-mode flag for agent_keepalive.sh
description: Follow-up to KEI-140 (PR #927 merged commit 1efc565d). Aiden + Max both flagged in review: aggressive 'git pull --rebase --autostash' on ExecStartPre is fail-open via '-' prefix but on feature branches with mid-build state, a rebase conflict leaves the agent booted in a conflicted state. Conservative-mode flag KEEPALIVE_PULL_MODE = ff-only | rebase | off would let operators tune per-callsign. Same shape as Atlas's pinned memory feedback_check_branch_before_checkout but applied at boot-time-pull rather than developer-time. Non-blocking; ship when fleet shape stabilises.
notes: Author: atlas. Suggested by Max review on PR #927 + Scout concur. Atlas+Aiden agree non-blocking on KEI-140 itself.

title: claim_source mechanical discriminator — Option B self-claim governance exemption
description:
3-way ratified Option B (Aiden+Max+Elliot ts \~1779022280) for the KEI-92 self-claim governance gap.

## What

Add `claim_source TEXT` column to public.tasks (values: 'auto_loop' | 'manual'). cmd_claim sets it based on caller. enforcer Rule 8 (DISPATCH-COORDINATION) + Step 0 gate (rule-kei-72) consult the column and SKIP enforcement when claim_source='auto_loop'.

## Why

Per GOV-12 (Gates As Code, Not Comments). Aiden's original Option B (exemption flag) refined by Max with mechanical-column discriminator: the data flag IS the gate, not a textual rule amendment that humans-must-remember.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Migration adds claim_source column with default 'manual' and NOT NULL after backfill
- [ ] cmd_claim --any path sets claim_source='auto_loop'
- [ ] cmd_claim --id path sets claim_source='manual'
- [ ] enforcer_deterministic.py Rule 8 reads task.claim_source and skips when 'auto_loop'
- [ ] rule-kei-72 Step 0 gate skipped on auto_loop tasks
- [ ] Backfill: existing rows default to 'manual' (conservative)
- [ ] Tests: auto_loop path no enforcer fire; manual path enforces as today
- [ ] KEI-108 gate PASS structural

## Pairing

Max (lead — tasks_cli + governance_rules context). \~30 LoC + 1 migration. Phase 0.5.

## Source

Dave KEI-deliberation 2026-05-17 \~12:50 UTC. 3-way ratified ts \~1779022280.</description>

title: KEI-32 audit — verify context_version.py 'cognee' version key still required
description: Audit finding: scripts/orchestrator/context_version.py line 27 has 'cognee' in the version-tracking dict. Per KEI-56 (discovery staleness governance), context_version tracks vendor versions for staleness detection. If Cognee is still vendor on the box, the key stays. If superseded by Weaviate, retire. Acceptance: confirm vendor inventory matches the dict — add 'weaviate' if missing; consider retiring 'cognee' if vendor is no longer queried from agent context.

title: KEI-32 audit — verify elliot_polling_loop.py cognee_ingest detection still needed
description: Audit finding: scripts/orchestrator/elliot_polling_loop.py lines 68, 73, 450 detect detached cognee_ingest subprocesses (anchor was Max's PID 844898 reparenting away from Claude). If cognee_ingest is no longer the primary memory ingest path (Weaviate indexers are), the polling-loop detection may be dead code. Acceptance: confirm whether cognee_ingest still runs as a long-lived subprocess; if not, retire the detection regex + 2 comments.

description:
Welcome to Linear! 

Watch an introductory video and access a list of resources below.

title: Connect your tools
description:
Integrations turn Linear into your source of truth around product development. Keep data in sync, and eliminate manual updates between tools.

![connect-your-tools.png](https://uploads.linear.app/fe63b3e2-bf87-46c0-8784-cd7d639287c8/c2eae035-37e2-4754-adcb-b8305431aa1f/c92d70c7-e6d0-4fa2-a0fd-78f6e780993a)

### **Key integrations**

* [**Slack**](<https://linear.app/settings/integrations/slack>)
  Create issues from Slack messages and sync threads
* [**GitHub**](<https://linear.app/settings/integrations/github>)** / **[**GitLab**](<https://linear.app/settings/integrations/gitlab>)
  Automate your pull request, commit workflows, and keep issues synced both ways
* [**Agents**](<https://linear.app/integrations/agents>)
  Deploy AI agents that work alongside you as teammates

### **Browse all integrations**

Discover 150+ available connections in our [integration directory](<https://linear.app/integrations>) – from bug creation via support tools (Intercom, Zendesk), to issues created from design explorations (Figma).

### **Linear API**

If you need something more custom, you can build directly on the Linear API (built on GraphQL). [See our Dev Docs to learn more](<https://linear.app/developers>).
